observations.reason: One project remained unimplemente d as of CY 2024.
Filling up of vacancy is still on going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that:
1.6.1 The Assistant Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review the
outstanding checks, regularly coordinate with MTO regarding stale
and cancelled checks, and record necessary adjustments in the
books of accounts in compliance with NGAS guidelines.
1.6.2 Henceforth, the Assistant Municipal Accountant ensure that any
stale checks as of year-end be reverted back to the Cash in Bank –
Local Currency, Current Account and the appropriate liability
accounts be recognized in order to correctly report the balances of
the said accounts in the statement of financial position as of year-
end.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Assistant Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to undertake the reconciliation of
the General Ledger and the Cashbook, on a regular basis, to ensure prompt,
accurate and complete recording of entries/data and fair presentation of
financial statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Assistant Municipal
Accountant submit all financial reports together with the supporting
documents within the prescribed period to the Auditor to effect the timely
review and evaluation of transactions as to their propriety, completeness and
accuracy.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed for:
4.10.1 The Engineering Office to submit a report of all completed projects
with attached Certificate of Completion to the Accounting Office as
the basis for recording to the appropriate PPE accounts; and
4.10.2 The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries
to reclassify the completed projects from the CIP accounts to the
proper PPE accounts and recognize the computed depreciation
thereof.

We recommended and the Management agreed for:
5.13.1 The Local Chief Executive issue an EO adopting the guidelines and
procedures of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE prescribed in COA
Circular 2020-006, and immediately implement the same to come up
with PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and
accountability; and
5.13.2 The General Services Office to gather and verify all necessary
documents to ensure that the RCPPE accurately reflects the correct
classification of PPE items.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Accounting Office
to:
6.7.1 Draw the following adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts
of the LGU to recognize the withholding taxes erroneously recorded as
Accounts Payable and ensure these are properly and timely
remitted to the BIR:
Accounts Payable xxx
Due to BIR xxx
6.7.2 Withhold and remit taxes on income payments to suppliers in
accordance with the proper timing of withholding of taxes as
prescribed under BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 47-2023
dated May 3, 2023.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Assistant Municipal
Accountant to draw the adjusting entry shown in the next page to capitalize
the borrowing costs to the proper PPE accounts.
Account Debit Credit
Construction in Progress - Buildings and Other
Structures 5,213,496.60
Government Equity 5,213,496.60
Henceforth, to capitalize borrowing costs directly attributable to the
construction of the Municipality’s infrastructure projects until its
completion.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Assistant Municipal
Accountant to:
8.5.1 Enforce the timely submission of liquidation reports to the Source
Agencies; and
8.5.2 Ensure strict compliance with Sections 4.9 and 6.7 of COA Circular
94-013 to prevent similar issues in the future.

observations.reason: Not Reiterated in Audit Observation
Not The reconciliation between GSO and Accounting’s records is still on going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to undertake the reconciliation of the General Ledger and the Cashbook, on a regular basis, to ensure prompt, accurate and complete recording of entries/data and fair presentation of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Continuous reconciliation between the Municipal Treasurer Office and the Municipal Accountant Office is being undertaken.
observations.reason: With high volume of reconciling items by the two books, the Management needs more time to fully reconcile the two records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Inventory Committee to expedite the completion of the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the Accounting Office records and require the MAccO to prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards to ensure that reported PPE balances are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.
observations.managementAction: Maintenance and reconciliation of RPCPPE with Accounting Office records is ongoing; the MAccO to prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards.
observations.reason: There is still a material difference of ₱151,370,759.18 between accounting and property records and the reconciliation is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to strongly enforce the submission of the liquidation reports by the NGOs/POs concerned and ensure the submission of complete and relevant documents in the request for write-off.
observations.managementAction: Demand Letter has been prepared but needs to explore other options to request for write-off since the POs can no longer be found including its place of business and offices.
observations.reason: The Management has prepared Demand Letter but needs to explore other options to write-off these long outstanding items as the POs can no longer be found.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that the projects to be funded under the 20% DF are properly planned and implemented and to exercise closer supervision and monitoring of the projects to ensure the timely completion of the projects.
observations.managementAction: The Local Government Prosperidad has already implemented measures and control to address the observation and conform with the recommendation under DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum No. 1.
observations.reason: There are still projects under 20% MDF that remained unimplemented and management needs to exercise measures and closer supervision and monitoring of the projects and ensure the timely completion of the projects.

observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended and the Management agreed for:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to only include released checks in the Monthly
Report of Check Issued for submission to Accounting Office; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to record the transaction only upon the receipt
of the RCI from the Treasury Office.

2.9 We recommended and the Management agreed for:
a. The Engineering Office to submit a report of all completed projects with
attached Certificate of Completion to the Accounting Office as basis for
recording to the appropriate PPE accounts; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to
reclassify the completed projects from the CIP accounts to the proper
PPE accounts and recognize the computed depreciation thereof.

3.7 We recommended and the Management agreed for The Municipal
Accountant to coordinate with the DPWH 1st DEO ADS Accounting Office
to secure the required documents in order to prepare adjusting entries and
record the transferred assets in the books of the Municipality under Road
Networks and Flood Control Systems accounts and compute the respective
depreciation.

4.6 We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal
Accounting Office to thoroughly review the contents of outstanding checks
and coordinate with the MTO that they will be furnished with the list of
checks staled and cancelled or checks staled but reissued together with the
original disbursement vouchers and other supporting documents to
facilitate accurate preparation of journal entry vouchers and ensure proper
recording of the same.

5.8 We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal
Accountant to immediately adjust the identified book reconciling items to
ensure that correct cash balances are presented in the Financial
Statements.

6.16 We recommended and the Management agreed for:
a. The General Services Office (GSO) to complete the reconciliation of the
RPCPPE with the Accounting Office records;
b. The Accounting Office to transfer the cost of completed projects booked as
PPE under the Trust Fund to the General Fund and record the
corresponding depreciation expenses in accordance with IPSAS 17; and
c. The Municipality to avail the one-time cleansing of non-existing/missing PPE
items granted to government agencies to ensure that reported PPE
balances are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

7.13 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Submit the lacking documents within one month from the receipt of this
AOM, otherwise, related transactions will be suspended/disallowed in
audit;
b. Ensure that expenses charged under the LDRRMF or any other
government funds shall be supported with complete and appropriate
documentation; and
c. Ensure the utilization of the LDRRMF is in accordance with the pertinent
laws, rules, and regulations to prevent possible invalidity of the
transactions.

8.10 We recommended and the Management agreed to rigidly monitor the
implementation of the projects for funds to be optimally utilized.

9.9 We recommended and the Management agreed to fast track the installation
of electrical power transformer so the plastic melter machine be put to
operation in CY 2024 in order to safeguard from wastage of resources and
to preclude the procurement transaction from being suspended or
disallowed in audit.

10.7 We recommended and the Management agreed to evaluate qualifications
of qualified personnel to fill up the mandatory positions and other
vacancies and/or consider hiring professionals or qualified graduates to
join the regular workforce of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed for:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to only include released checks in the Monthly Report of Check Issued for submission to Accounting Office; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to record the transaction only upon the receipt of the RCI from the Treasury Office.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has commissioned the Local Finance for the formulation of a policy unto this effect. Thus, the Municipal Treasury Office will only be preparing check for transaction suppliers are ready to claim or being ready to claim and/or by follow-up to avoid long time and the outstanding checks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Treasury Office and the Municipal Accounting Office to take up the necessary documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasury Office has updated the schedule of domestic loan and has reconciled the loan balances based on available documents.
observations.reason: There is still no current portion of the Loans Payable - Domestic account as at year end; the loan balances are all classified as noncurrent.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to record proper adjusting entries of previously identified book reconciling items aggregating ₱5,562,189.05 to prevent accumulation over time affecting proper valuation of the cash account.
observations.managementAction: Verification of data has been done and proper adjusting entries has been recorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed for the Accountable Officer (AO) to immediately take up the long outstanding deposits in transit as a receivable from the accountable officer and to reflect the accurate balance of the Cash in Bank.
observations.managementAction: DIT was found as mis-deposited entries on other accounts and was booked up as receivable from the concerned funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed for:
a. The General Services Office (GSO) to prepare and submit regularly on a monthly basis the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issuance, backed with the supply issuance report and generation of the supply system; 
b. The Accounting Office to properly record the purchases of inventories in the appropriate accounts and the consumption thereof as expense and maintain perpetual inventory records; and
c. The GSO to reconcile the same with report from Accounting Office, and effect appropriate adjustments to reflect the accurate balance of the Inventory accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Maintenance of supply ledger card has been embedded in the eNGAS system of the Accounting office and the GSO. The GSO has been instructed to maintain a record of inventories and supply issuance and the Accounting Office records the purchases.
observations.reason: There is still no reconciliation between GSO and Accounting’s records is still on going.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to submit all bank statements, monthly trial balance and disbursement vouchers together with the supporting documents within the prescribed period to the Auditor to effect the timely review and evaluation of the propriety, completeness and accuracy of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: All DV's, monthly trial balance, and supporting documents have been transmitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO to comply with the provisions of Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and timely submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Management has submitted the required reports to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to maintain subsidiary ledgers for the agency’s DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer, transfer of other funds and other sources as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Management has submitted the DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer and maintained appropriate records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to institutionalize the creation of the PDAO by taking the following course of actions: a.) Human Resource Management Office (HRMO), with the assistance of the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO), to exert effort in hiring qualified members of PWDs to fill-in the created positions; and b.) Budget Officer to allocate budget for PS and CO, if necessary, for the operation of the office.
observations.managementAction: LGU Prosperidad has already created the Office of the Persons with Disability by virtue of an ordinance. This also, creates proper staffing pattern for operation pursuant to RA 10070.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Local School Board to ensure that there is appropriation under the SEF funds for the implementation of the ECCD Programs in accordance with RA 10410 & RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board has already made appropriation for ECCD PPAs under the SEF funds for implementation of the Program pursuant to RA 10410 & RA 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Accounting Unit and the Inventory Committee to reconcile the PPE accounts and effect the necessary adjustments in their respective records. Require the Municipal Accountant to make an adjustment to record the PPE under the Trust Fund and consequently transfer the same to the General Fund and compute the corresponding depreciation and to take up the cost of the unrecorded 6 parcels of land with an assessed value of ₱4,606,880.00.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: 1.7 We recommended that the Management direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a comprehensive physical count of all types of inventories and reconcile with the records of the Accounting Office. Any deficiency should be investigated and effect the necessary adjustments and submit the inventory report to the Auditor as prescribed in Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Accounting Unit and the GSO to reconcile the PPE accounts and effect the necessary adjustments in their respective records. Require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the completed infrastructure projects and other PPEs from trust fund to the general fund and compute the corresponding depreciation.

We recommended that the Management direct the Accountant to submit immediately the monthly trial balance and disbursement vouchers to the Office of the Auditor. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer to return to the Accounting Office all paid vouchers immediately after payment has been made in order for the latter to have ample time to record the transactions in the books.  Strictly comply to the requirements on the submission of transaction documents and financial reports pursuant to Item 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Sec. 70 of Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs).

4.7 We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to verify with the source agency on the existence and correctness of the dormant accounts and subsequently remit the correct amount to the National Treasury. Likewise, immediately return the remaining balances of the completed projects and submit the necessary liquidation reports to the source agencies.

5.7 Require the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letter to the Barangays concerned to immediately liquidate the fund transfers through submission of Fund Utilization Report supported with the necessary documents and advise the Barangay to refund the amount in case the Barangay failed to implement the project within the timeframe pursuant to the provision of the MOA. Likewise, direct the officer in-charge to conduct regular monitoring of project implementation and provide the needed assistance/interventions, if warranted to ensure prompt completion of the projects.

We recommended that the Management require the office concerned to specify in the Activity Design the number and function of the persons who will participate in vaccination process, thereby limiting the meal expenses to those who are actually facilitating the activity and thus maximizing the use of COVID-19 Fund. Assign personnel to facilitate the attendance sheet in order to ensure that only those persons involved are the ones getting the food packs and sign in the form.

7.8   We recommended that the Office concerned ensure that all trip tickets are completely filled-out and provided with specific public purpose. Discontinue allowing personal-purpose travels to avoid incurrence of irregular expenditures. Likewise, to stop charging expenses not related to the mandate and function of the offices concerned and consider realignment of the funds and place it to the office directly catering to the health needs of the constituents.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the BRS regularly and make adjustments in all valid reconciling items to present a reliable cash in bank balance in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting continuously investigated the existing reconciling items
observations.reason: Some reconciling items remained unadjusted and details of debit and credit memos were unknown.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management prioritize the titling of all land owned by the LGU to assure absolute ownership over these properties to avoid third party claims and to ensure that the LGU has legal rights over all recognized assets in the financial statements.
observations.reason: On-going processing of the land title

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat to submit to the Office of the Auditor the perfected contracts of the 37 projects together with their complete supporting documents for our evaluation. Strictly observe the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2019, for timely conduct of auditorial and technical review.

We recommended that the management instruct:
The officers concerned to submit to the Supply Officer the complete RDS for the 15,688 sacks of rice and other documents to prove that the medicines, PPE and other supplies were received by the identified recipients/beneficiaries;

The Supply Officer to verify the completeness of the RDS and distribution list for the medicines and other supplies, any discrepancy should be investigated to established accountability and to prepare the RSMI for submission to the Accounting Officer; and

The Municipal Accountant to book the remaining balance of the goods to the related inventory accounts, if any and submit to the Office of the Auditor the revised RSMI and its supporting documents. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of all goods and materials, whether for stock or for distribution, under the inventory account, regardless of its c and charges pursuant to Section 184 and 134 of the GAM, Volume I and recognize the expense upon receipt of the RSMI and its supporting documents to ensure that all recorded expenses are supported with proper and complete documentation.

We recommended that Management follow the provisions set under COA Circular No. 2020-09 dated April 21, 2020 regarding the reporting and accounting guidelines of cash and in-kind donations. Furnish the Office of the Auditor the required Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balance for all donations received together with the complete items provide assurance that the aids and donations received were properly accounted for.
observations.reason: Some in-kind donations were not reflected in the reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the BAC to secure the lacking supporting documents from the suppliers concerned and submit the same to the Auditor for further evaluation. Henceforth, emergency purchases of the LGU should be supported with the basic documents prescribed under GPPB Circular 01-2020.
observations.reason: Some emergency purchases were not supported with the required Mayor’s Permit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 01 dated March 27. 2020 on the utilization of the COVID-19 fund, which main intent is to provide basic services to the affected populace brought about by COVID-19.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions stated under Administrative (AO) Nos. 226, s. 2020 in granting COVID Hazard pay to its employees to avoid incurrence of excessive expenditure.
We recommended that management discontinue the designation of personnel form the Office of the Accounting as TWG of the BAC to maintain segregation of functions and to avoid conflict of interest.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the balance of the unutilized DRRMF from Due to LGUs account to Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and henceforth use the latter account in recording the yearly unspent DRRMF in the Trust Fund pursuant to Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that management truly assess further the Operation of the Motorpool to avoid misconception that it was established to accommodate bloated labor force and evade the budgetary limitation on personnel services as its income is still greatly dependent on implemented projects by administration.
observations.managementAction: Majority of the income still relies on LGUs projects implemented by administration.
observations.reason: The Motorpool still rely on the income from projects implemented by administration.

observations.recommendation: 1.10 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the BRS regularly and make adjustments in all valid reconciling items to present a reliable cash in bank balance in the financial statement.

We recommended that Management instruct the Accounting Unit and the Inventory Committee to reconcile the PPE accounts and effect the necessary adjustments in their respective records. Require the Municipal Accountant to make an adjustment to record the PPE under the Trust Fund and consequently transfer the same to the General Fund and compute the corresponding depreciation and to take up the cost of the unrecorded 6 parcels of land with an assessed value of ₱4,606,880.00.

3.8 We recommended that the Management prioritize the titling of all land owned by the LGU to assure absolute ownership over these properties to avoid third party claims and to ensure that the LGU has legal rights over all recognized assets in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat to submit to the Office of the Auditor the perfected contracts of the 37 projects together with their complete supporting documents for our evaluation. Strictly observe the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, for timely conduct of auditorial and technical review.

5.7  We recommended that Management instruct:
The officers concerned to submit to the Supply Officer the complete RDS for the 15,688 sacks of rice and other documents to prove that the medicines, PPEs and other goods were received by the identified recipients/beneficiaries;
The Supply Officer to verify the completeness of the RDS and distribution list for the medicines and other supplies, any discrepancy should be investigated to establish accountability and to prepare the RSMI for submission to the Accounting Office; and
The Municipal Accountant to book the remaining balance of the goods to the related inventory accounts, if any and submit to the Office of the Auditor the verified RSMI and its supporting documents. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of all goods and materials, whether for stock or for distribution, under the inventory account, regardless of its charges pursuant to Section 188 and 134 of the GAM, Volume I and recognize the expense upon receipt of the RSMI and its supporting documents to ensure that all recorded expenses are supported with proper and complete documentation.

We recommended that Management follow the provisions set under COA Circular No. 2020-09 dated April 21, 2020 regarding the reporting and accounting guidelines of cash and in-kind donations. Furnish the Office of the Auditor the required Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for all donations received together with the complete Acknowledgement Receipts, RDS and inventory of the remaining undistributed items to provide assurance that the aids and donations received were properly accounted for.

We recommended that the Management instruct the BAC to secure the lacking supporting documents from the suppliers concerned and submit the same to the Auditor for further evaluation. Henceforth, emergency purchases of the LGU should be supported with the basic documents prescribed under GPPB Circular 01-2020.

8.6 We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 01 dated March 27, 2020 on the utilization of the COVID-19 fund, which main intent is to provide basic services to the affected populace brought about by COVID-19.

9.7 We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions stated under Administrative Order (AO) Nos. 26, s. 2020 in granting COVID Hazard pay to its employees to avoid incurrence of excessive expenditure.

10.4 We recommended that Management discontinue the designation of personnel from the Office of the Accounting as TWG of the BAC to maintain segregation of functions and to avoid conflict of interest.

11.5 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the balance of the unutilized DRRMF from Due to LGUs account to Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and henceforth use the latter account in recording the yearly unspent DRRMF in the Trust Fund pursuant to Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

12.3  The Management was encouraged to continue to monitor the implementation of all on-going projects and ensure its completion as planned in order to attain the desired benefits as envisioned in the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of DILG and DBM.

We recommended that the Management strictly comply with Section 305 of the LGC and all its other provisions pertaining to Local Fiscal Administration to preclude future disallowance of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: All disbursement from 20% DF were supported with appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management implement immediately all pending programs and projects and submit a status report to the source agency and subsequently return any unspent balances of completed projects in compliance to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 and the Memorandum of Agreement.
observations.managementAction: Delayed implementation due to COVID pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fast tracks the implementation of all unimplemented and partially implemented projects embodied in the Development Plan of CY 2019. Further, adequate planning should always be undertaken, before procurement is carried out to prevent incurrence of unnecessary delays during implementation of infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: 20% Local Development Fund was optimally utilized this CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board carefully evaluates the programs/projects to be included in the SEF Budget prioritizing the capital expenditures or investment with long term benefits taking into consideration the objectives set forth under existing laws and regulations that govern the utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: SEF Budget prioritizing the investment with long term benefits.
observations.reason: Disbursements for catering services/meals and snacks reached to Php862,080.00 or 35% of the total appropriation for the Special Education Fund, giving less priority on the programs/projects with long term benefits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management complies on the requirements of Budget Circular No. 2019-5 and submits the required documents enumerated above for the auditor’s validation in order to preclude disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Complied with all the requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly enforce for the liquidation of the cash advances and to regulate the granting of additional cash advances especially to those who still have unsettled cash advances.
observations.managementAction: On-going liquidation of cash advances
observations.reason: Some AOs still failed to settle their unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended that the Management strictly comply with Section 305
of the LGC and all its other provisions pertaining to Local Fiscal
Administration to preclude future disallowance of the transactions.

2.10 We recommended that the management revisits and evaluates/assesses
the guidelines and operation of the motorpool and decide what strategy
is more viable to achieve realizable income from outside source without
defeating the purpose of its creation.

3.7 We recommended that the Management implement immediately all
pending programs and projects and submit a status report to the source
agency and subsequently return any unspent balances of completed
projects in compliance to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 and
the Memorandum of Agreement.

4.6 We recommended that the Management fast tracks the implementation
of all unimplemented and partially implemented projects embodied in
the Development Plan of CY 2019. Further, adequate planning should
always be undertaken, before procurement is carried out to prevent
incurrence of unnecessary delays during implementation of
infrastructure projects.

5.4 We recommended that the Management strictly enforce for the
liquidation of the cash advances and to regulate the granting of
additional cash advances especially to those who still have unsettled cash
advances.

6.7 We recommended that the Management directs the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments
to reflect the correct account balances thereby presenting these accounts
in the financial statements accurately and fairly.
We recommend that then amount of 20% DF be returned as the purpose of the transfer is not capital expenditure in nature.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommend for the immediate submission of the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) to the Office of the Auditor for timely review and evaluation of bank balances as to their accuracy pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that Management strictly enforce for the
liquidation of the cash advances and to regulate the granting of additional
cash advances especially to those who still have unsettled cash advances.

We recommended for Management to be conscious of the  legal  deadline
of the submission of  the  year-end  reports  as  its  non-submission
affects the audit opinion to be rendered on the said reports.

We recommended that adequate planning should always be undertaken,
before procurement  are  carried  out  and  projects  are implemented, to
prevent incurrence of unnecessary delays during implementation.

We recommended that adequate planning should always be undertaken,
before procurement  are  carried  out  and  projects  are implemented, to
prevent incurrence of unnecessary delays during implementation.

We recommended  that the management truly  assesses  further  the
Operation of the  Motorpool  to  avoid  misconception  that  it  was
established  to  accommodate  bloated  labor  force  and  evade  the
budgetary limitation on personnel services.

We recommended that management fast track the implementation of the
programs and projects funded out  of  the  downloaded  funds  so
intended beneficiaries could enjoy the benefit the projects has to offer
to them.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipality  adopts  a  disaster
management approach  that  is  holistic  and  proactive  to  prevent
aggravating adverse impacts of disasters and if possible lessen the
occurrence of disaster other than the natural one.

We recommended that management also  prioritize  allocation  of
budget for carrying out the programs, projects and activities in the
approved Solid Waste Management Plan in compliance to RA 9003.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the conversion of the controlled dumpsite to a sanitary landfill as the final disposal facility of the LGU’s solid waste in compliance to RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with our recommendation.
observations.reason: No action taken by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly follow the fundamental principles in governing financial affairs, transactions and operations of the local government unit with emphasis on Section 305 (a) of the Local Government Code.

We recommend that Management strictly follow the fundamental principles in governing financial affairs, transactions and operations of the local government unit with emphasis on Section 305 (a) of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement regularly and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for timely review and evaluation of bank balances as to their accuracy pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The management promised to submit the lacking Bank Reconciliation Staements before March 15, 2017.
observations.reason: No action taken by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prioritize the allocation of budget for carrying out the programs, projects and activities in the approved Solid Waste Management Plan. Further, to continuously informed the constituents of the importance of waste management through IEC and for the LGU to adopt specific revenue-generating measures to support the sustainability of its Solid Waste Management Plan if it lacks funds to implement it.
We recommended that Management appropriate funds, of what is required  by law, for the strengthening and implementation of programs of the LCPC.
We recommended that Management directs all officers and employees to strictly adhere with rules and regulations on the liquidation of cash advances.

We recommend that Management prohibit the practice of signing a check in advance or in blank to avoid exposure of government funds to misuse or misappropriation and for management to install, implement and monitor sound system of internal control in compliance with Sec. 124 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that management directs the General Services and Accounting Offices to reconcile its records so that necessary adjustment could be affected on either the accounting or property records.

We reiterated our prior recommendation that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement religiously and regularly, and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for timely review and evaluation of bank balances as to their accuracy pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We therefore recommend for the immediate submission of the disbursement vouchers, official receipts, payrolls and other reports pertaining to the months of February 2017 to September 2017. Likewise, please submit the lacking documents for the month of January 2017 as enumerated in the attached schedule (Annex G) to enable us to make decision in audit.

We recommend that Management ensures the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. Further, to also avoid charging ineligible expenditures to the fund to preclude incurrence of disallowance.

We recommend that management assesses further the operation of the Motorpool so it will not be construed that it was only established for administrative reason which is to accommodate a bloated LGU labor force.

We recommended that LDRRMC include in the plan only those considered disaster risk management related activities and to observed the proper utilization of the LDRRM Fund to preclude future audit disallowance..
observations.managementAction: Observation and recommendation will be taken religiously by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for Management to ensure that the 20% Development Fund be optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes as it is the responsibility of every local chief executive to do so and to remind management that utilizing the fund for any purpose beyond those expressly prescribed by law or public policy could be subject to sanctions provided under the Local Government Code.

We recommended that Management, through the local finance committee, take the necessary safety nets relative to availing credit facilities to preclude occurrence of needing to allocate a lion’s share of their Internal Revenue Allotment to repay loans leaving little funds for the delivery of essential public services.
observations.managementAction: Action taken by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management strictly adhere to the provisions of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002
observations.managementAction: Management has agreed to strictly impose the provisions stated in the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office to closely monitor the undertaking of proposed projects/programs funded out of MDRRMF to institutionalize measures for reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all levels. Furthermore, we recommended that the concerned Municipal Budget Officer to strictly adhere to the provisions stated under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to exhaust all available remedies provided under the law, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance of the amount of disallowance/charges due from them and strictly observe the provisions of Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-06. Further, properly maintain the records of the disallowances for updates and monitoring.

We recommend for the Management to instruct the General Service Office to conduct the inventory taking of the PPE so that the Accounting Unit can easily reconcile with their recorded PPEs to come up with the accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Assessor and other officials concerned to undertake effective, efficient, and aggressive tax collection strategies to improve collection. For the delinquent real property taxes, we recommend the management to impose the legal remedies either by administrative auction thru levy on real property or judicial auction to enforce collection.
observations.managementAction: The Treasurer has agreed to comply with our recommendation to enforce the legal remedies to enforce collection.

observations.recommendation: Direct Management to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the BRS regularly and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
We recommend to the Management to instruct the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile the records so as to come up with the accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.
We recommend for the Management to properly scrutinize the transactions and require valid and legal complete documents to support the claims pursuant to Sec. 4(6) 0f P.D 1445.

We recommend to the Management to direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit  financial reports together with the supporting documents within the prescribed period of submission for timely review and evaluation of transactions as to their propriety, completeness and accuracy and to point out to management whatever defects found thereon for corrective measures.

Enjoin Management to prioritize those programs, projects and activities stipulated in RA 7160.
Henceforth, Utilities, Construction Materials, Office and Medicine supplies for District use will be charged to their respective appropriations.

Require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare and submit to the GPPB the procurement monitoring report within the time frame stipulated in Section 12.2 of the Amended IRR-A of RA 9184 so that monitoring and evaluation of the LGU’s compliance to the provisions of the Procurement Act can be made.
Enjoin Management to strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations by faithfully executing disqualification on the unqualified bidders in accordance with RA 9184.
We recommend that Management prioritize the conversion of the controlled dumpsite to a sanitary landfill as the final disposal facility of the LGU’s solid waste in compliance to RA 9003.

2.4 We recommend for Management to ensure that the 20% Development Fund be optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes as it is the responsibility of every local chief executive to do so and to remind management that utilizing the fund for any purpose beyond those expressly prescribed by law or public policy could be subject to sanctions provided under the Local Government Code.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement regularly and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for timely review and evaluation of bank balances as to their accuracy pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

5.6  We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to exhaust all available remedies provided under the law, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance of the amount of disallowance/charges due from them and strictly observe the provisions of Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-06. Further, properly maintain the records of the disallowances for updates and monitoring.

6.4   We recommend the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Assessor and other officials concerned to undertake effective, efficient, and aggressive tax collection strategies to improve collection. For the delinquent real property taxes, we recommend the management to impose the legal remedies either by administrative auction thru levy on real property or judicial auction to enforce collection.

7.5  We recommend for the Management to instruct the General Service Office to conduct the inventory taking of the PPE so that the Accounting Unit can easily reconcile with their recorded PPEs to come up with the accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

8.4 We recommend that LDRRMC include in the plan only those considered disaster risk management related activities and to observed the proper utilization of the LDRRM Fund to preclude future audit disallowance.

9.7 We recommend that Management, through the local finance committee, take the necessary safety nets relative to availing credit facilities to preclude occurrence of needing to allocate a lion’s share of their Internal Revenue Allotment to repay loans leaving little funds for the delivery of essential public services.

10.8 We recommend for the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office to closely monitor the undertaking of proposed projects/programs funded out of MDRRMF to institutionalize measures for reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all levels. Furthermore, we recommended that the concerned Municipal Budget Officer to strictly adhere to the provisions stated under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to properly supervise all the Accountable Officers and he should see to it that in the issuance of the official receipts, all copies of each receipt shall be the exact copies or carbon reproductions in all respects of the original pursuant to Section 73 of the General Accounting and Audit Manual and No. 24, Chapter II of the Revised Cash Examination Manual.

We recommend that Management should discontinue the maintenance of the current account with FCB and transfer the said account to an AGDB or to the general fund account of the LGU, unless prior approval from the DOF will be secured by the Municipal Treasurer as mentioned in the foregoing regulations.

We recommend that all collectors shall prepare the RAAF to facilitate review by the Municipal Treasurer as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issuances and ending balances on hand.  In turn, the Municipal Treasurer should prepare the CRAAF which should be submitted to the Auditor for review and verification every month.

We recommend to the Management to direct the Accountant to record the Drugs and Medicines to expense account upon receipt of documentary requirements such as Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and Report of Utilization.

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit all DVs to the Audit Team within the reglementary period provided under COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 in order to establish the propriety of the affected accounts, otherwise, the same will be suspended in audit.

We recommend to the Management to prepare a GAD Plan and GAD Accomplishment Report in order to fulfill the mandate on programs and activities geared towards gender equity by prioritizing women focused activities.
Direct Management to strictly monitor the project implementation to ensure that the said projects be finished on a timely manner and be utilized immediately by the public and tourists.

We recommend to the Management to install controls and strictly monitor the implementations of the programs and projects under EDP. Early planning and evaluation should be done to avoid inclusion of lump-sum appropriations in the budget.
